title: Production Support
description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Investigate and resolve production incidents based on defect severity and SLA commitments.

  • Act as the primary point of contact for production issues and user queries, ensuring timely communication and resolution.

  • Log, track, and manage incidents, problems, and service requests through ITSM tools, following issues through to closure and meeting incident closure KPIs.

  • Ensure system availability and stability in line with agreed SLAs and operational standards.

  • Monitor and validate Start of Day (SOD) and End of Day (EOD) batch processing for supported applications.

  • Support and participate in annual Disaster Recovery (DR) exercises, ensuring application readiness and compliance.

  • Coordinate with system and infrastructure teams during server patching, upgrades, and maintenance activities.

  • Perform proactive monitoring using third-party tools such as BMC Control-M, Geneos, Splunk, and SQL Monitor.

  • Support market data monitoring and assist with Geneos dashboard configuration and alert tuning.

  • Maintain accurate incident, change, and operational documentation for each production release.

  • Provide effective workarounds under strict time constraints using AS400/Mainframe, Unix/Linux shell scripting, and database scripts.

  • Ensure operational readiness by updating runbooks, SOPs, and support documentation.

  • Collaborate across teams while demonstrating a proactive, customer-focused, and professional approach.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Strong experience in Application / Production Support within 24/7 environments.

  • Hands-on expertise with AS400, Unix/Linux Shell scripting, Oracle, MS SQL, and BMC Control-M Scheduler.

  • Experience with batch processing, job scheduling, and monitoring.

  • Familiarity with ITIL Service Operations (Incident, Problem, Change Management).

  • Ability to perform under pressure with excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

    Disclaimer: The company is committed to ensuring the privacy and security of your information. By submitting this form, you consent to the collection, processing, and retention of the information you provide. The data collected (which may include your contact details, educational background, work experience and skills) will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating your qualifications for the position you're applying for. Your data will be stored securely and retained for the duration necessary to fulfill our hiring process. If you are not selected for the position, your data will be kept on file for a limited period in case future opportunities arise. You have the right to access, correct, or delete your data at any time by contacting us at Quess Singapore | A Leading Staffing Services Provider in Singapore (quesscorp.sg)

    This is in partnership with the Employment and Employability Institute Pte Ltd (“e2i”).

    e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions. e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives. By applying for this role, you consent to Quesscorp Singapore’s PDPA and e2i’s PDPA
